target.title: fix(ui): prevent duplicate assistant messages when session.message races with chat final
target.body:
## Summary

When a plugin registers a `before_agent_finalize` hook that blocks streaming, `session.message` and `chat final` can arrive in an unexpected order, causing both paths to write to `chatMessages` concurrently — producing duplicate assistant messages in webchat.

This PR establishes a single-terminal-owner model: only `chat final/error/aborted` owns ending the local chat run and reconciling transcript state. `session.message` during an active run only records a `pendingTranscriptSync` flag; the terminal handler consumes it after the final message is committed, avoiding concurrent writes to `chatMessages`.

- Establishes `chat final/error/aborted` as the sole owner of local run termination and transcript reconciliation
- `session.message` during an active run records `pendingTranscriptSync` and returns immediately — no `loadChatHistory`, `loadSessions`, or run reconciliation
- Terminal handler consumes pending sync at most once, after committing the final message
- Removes previous workarounds: text-signature dedup, `chatRunId` guards, polling retry mechanisms, and all `[DEDUPE]` debug logs

## Linked context

Closes # (no existing issue)

Related to internal design doc at `docs/duplicate-message-ordering-design.md`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Duplicate assistant messages in webchat when a `before_agent_finalize` hook defers terminal delivery
- Real environment tested: macOS, source-built gateway on port 18789
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Plugin with `before_agent_finalize` hook registered
  2. Send a chat message
  3. Observe webchat UI — only one assistant reply appears
- Evidence after fix: Webchat shows a single assistant message per turn; no duplicate entries in chatMessages
- Observed result after fix: No duplicate messages across multiple test runs with the hook scenario
- What was not tested: Multi-client scenario; subagent `session.message` sources; transcript injection paths
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Tested on single-client macOS setup

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts` — 35 tests pass
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` — 119 tests pass
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` — 77 tests pass
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` — 33 tests pass
- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts` — 170 tests pass
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` — 68 tests pass
- `pnpm build:strict-smoke` — passes
- `pnpm ui:build` — passes

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- 7 new test cases in `app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts` covering: session.message before/after chat final, multiple session.messages coalescing, old-run terminal not consuming new-run pending sync, session-switch guard
- 2 new regression tests in `chat.test.ts` verifying same-text different-turn messages are both preserved, and no text-only dedup occurs

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Duplicate assistant messages appeared when a `before_agent_finalize` hook deferred terminal delivery, causing `session.message` to arrive before `chat final`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes — eliminates duplicate assistant messages

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No

What is the highest-risk area?
Event ordering invariants. The fix assumes `chat final` always eventually arrives as the terminal owner. If a `chat final` is never delivered (e.g., gateway crash mid-run), `pendingTranscriptSync` would never be consumed.

How is that risk mitigated?
Gateway lifecycle broadcasts (`chat final → sessions.changed phase:end`) are synchronous on the same WebSocket connection. The `sessions.changed` path still performs reconciliation as a safety net for runs without a local owner. This PR only restricts `session.message` from reconciling during active runs.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
Awaiting reviewer feedback

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
All local tests pass; awaiting CI and maintainer review
target.author: scotthuang
